description: A macroeconomist needs to test the permanent income hypothesis by determining if consumption and income are cointegrated using quarterly US macroeconomic data from FRED located in /home/ga/Documents/gretl_data/ ('PCEC96.csv' and 'DSPIC96.csv'). Import the data into Gretl, create log-transformed time-series variables (lcons = log(PCEC96) and linc = log(DSPIC96)), and perform Augmented Dickey-Fuller (ADF) unit root tests on both levels and first-differences. Conduct an Engle-Granger two-step cointegration test between lcons and linc, estimating the long-run relationship and testing the resulting residuals for stationarity. Save all test results and the cointegrating regression output to /home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/cointegration_results.txt.

metadata.data_source: US Bureau of Economic Analysis via FRED (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis)
